  "text": "The HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 header normalization and translation logic in envoyproxy/envoy (v1.31.2) is spread across several layers:\n\n## 1. Header Case Normalization (Lowercase)\n\n**`envoy/http/header_map.h`** defines `LowerCaseString`, the core type that enforces lowercase normalization on all header names. Its constructor calls `lower()` which uses `absl::ascii_tolower` on every character. All internal header storage uses this type.\n\n**`source/common/http/header_map_impl.cc`** and `header_map_impl.h` implement `HeaderMapImpl`, which stores all headers using `LowerCaseString` keys, enforcing lowercase at the storage layer.\n\n**`source/common/http/http1/header_formatter.cc`** provides `ProperCaseHeaderKeyFormatter::format()`, which capitalizes HTTP/1.1 header names for outbound H1 responses (e.g., `content-type` → `Content-Type`). This is the inverse: lowercased keys are re-cased for H1 wire format.\n\n**`source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc`** (`ConnectionManagerUtility::mutateRequestHeaders`) calls `setScheme(absl::AsciiStrToLower(...))` to normalize the `:scheme` pseudo-header to lowercase.\n\n**`source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http2_header_validator.cc`** (`Http2HeaderValidator::validateGenericHeaderName`) enforces RFC 9113's requirement that HTTP/2 header names MUST be all lowercase (rejects uppercase characters in range 0x41–0x5a).\n\n## 2. Pseudo-Header Injection (:method, :path, :scheme, :authority)\n\n**`source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc`** is the primary site where HTTP/1.1 request headers are parsed and pseudo-headers injected:\n- `ServerConnectionImpl::handlePath()` (line ~1100): Sets `:path` from the request-target, `:scheme` (lowercased via `absl::AsciiStrToLower`) from absolute URLs, and `:authority` (via `setHost()`, which maps to `:authority` in Envoy's internal representation) from the Host header or absolute URL host.\n- `ServerConnectionImpl::onHeadersCompleteBase()` (line ~1186): Calls `headers->setMethod(parser_->methodName())` to inject `:method`, calls `handlePath()` to inject `:path`/`:scheme`/`:authority`, then validates with `HeaderUtility::requestHeadersValid()`.\n- `StreamEncoderImpl::encodeHeadersBase()` (line ~138): When encoding H2 headers back to H1 wire format, translates `:authority` → `host` (skips other pseudo-headers starting with `:`).\n\n**`source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc`** (`ServerConnectionImpl::onHeader`, line ~2306): Handles the special case where both `host` and `:authority` are present—discards `host` if `:authority` already set.\n\n**`source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc`** (`ConnectionManagerUtility::mutateRequestHeaders`): Sets `:scheme` if missing, based on `X-Forwarded-Proto` or TLS status, and normalizes it to lowercase.\n\n**`source/common/http/header_utility.cc`** (`HeaderUtility::checkRequiredRequestHeaders`, `requestHeadersValid`): Validates the presence and validity of `:method`, `:path`, `:authority` pseudo-headers.\n\n**`source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http1_header_validator.cc`** and **`http2_header_validator.cc`**: The UHV (Universal Header Validator) layer validates pseudo-headers (`:method`, `:authority`, `:scheme`, `:path`) for both H1 and H2 requests.\n\n## 3. Upgrade Header Handling (H1↔H2 Translation)\n\n**`source/common/http/utility.cc`** and **`utility.h`** contain the core upgrade transformation functions:\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeRequestFromH1toH2()`: Converts H1 `Upgrade: <protocol>` → H2 extended CONNECT (sets `:method: CONNECT`, `:protocol: <upgrade-value>`, removes `Upgrade` and `Connection` headers).\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeResponseFromH1toH2()`: Converts H1 `101 Switching Protocols` → H2 `200` response, removes `Upgrade`/`Connection`.\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeRequestFromH2toH1()`: Reverses the above—converts H2 extended CONNECT back to H1 `Upgrade` request.\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeResponseFromH2toH1()`: Converts H2 `200` extended CONNECT response back to H1 `101 Switching Protocols`.\n- `Utility::isUpgrade()`, `Utility::isH2UpgradeRequest()`: Detection helpers.\n\n**`source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc`**: Calls these transform functions in `ClientStreamImpl::encodeHeaders()` (H1→H2 direction when sending upstream) and `ServerStreamImpl::decodeHeaders()` (H2→H1 direction when receiving from upstream).\n\n**`source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc`**: Handles upgrade detection (`handling_upgrade_`), blocks h2c upgrade requests, and controls chunk encoding bypass for upgraded connections.\n\n**`source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc`** (`mutateRequestHeaders`): Preserves `Connection` and `Upgrade` headers for upgrade requests (skips hop-by-hop header removal).\n\n**`source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http2_header_validator.cc`**: The UHV layer performs the H1↔H2 upgrade transformation:\n- `ServerHttp2HeaderValidator::transformRequestHeaders()`: Calls `transformUpgradeRequestFromH2toH1()` for incoming H2 extended CONNECT.\n- `ServerHttp2HeaderValidator::transformResponseHeaders()`: Calls `transformUpgradeResponseFromH1toH2()` for outgoing responses.\n- `ClientHttp2HeaderValidator::transformRequestHeaders()`: Calls `transformUpgradeRequestFromH1toH2()` for outgoing H1 upgrade requests going to H2 upstream.\n- `ClientHttp2HeaderValidator::transformResponseHeaders()`: Calls `transformUpgradeResponseFromH2toH1()` for responses coming back from H2 upstream."

# Envoy HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 Header Normalization Migration

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that handle HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 header translation: the header case normalization (lowercase), the pseudo-header injection (:method, :path, :scheme, :authority), and the upgrade header handling.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.


## Output Format

Now I have a complete picture. Let me write the answer:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Summary of Findings

### 1. Header Case Normalization (Lowercase)

- **`envoy/http/header_map.h`** — `LowerCaseString` class: enforces lowercase on all header names via `absl::ascii_tolower` in its constructor
- **`source/common/http/header_map_impl.cc`** — `HeaderMapImpl`: stores all headers using `LowerCaseString` keys
- **`source/common/http/http1/header_formatter.cc`** — `ProperCaseHeaderKeyFormatter::format()`: re-capitalizes headers for H1 wire format output
- **`source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc`** — normalizes `:scheme` to lowercase via `absl::AsciiStrToLower`
- **`source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http2_header_validator.cc`** — `validateGenericHeaderName()`: rejects uppercase characters per RFC 9113

### 2. Pseudo-Header Injection (:method, :path, :scheme, :authority)

- **`source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc`** — `ServerConnectionImpl::handlePath()` injects `:path`, `:scheme`, `:authority`; `onHeadersCompleteBase()` injects `:method`; `StreamEncoderImpl::encodeHeadersBase()` translates `:authority` → `host` for H1 output
- **`source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc`** — `ServerConnectionImpl::onHeader()`: handles host/:authority deduplication
- **`source/common/http/header_utility.cc`** — validates presence/validity of required pseudo-headers

### 3. Upgrade Header Handling (H1↔H2)

- **`source/common/http/utility.cc`** — core transform functions: `transformUpgradeRequestFromH1toH2()`, `transformUpgradeResponseFromH1toH2()`, `transformUpgradeRequestFromH2toH1()`, `transformUpgradeResponseFromH2toH1()`
- **`source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc`** — calls these in `ClientStreamImpl::encodeHeaders()` and `ServerStreamImpl::decodeHeaders()`
- **`source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http2_header_validator.cc`** — UHV layer: `ServerHttp2HeaderValidator::transformRequestHeaders/ResponseHeaders()` and `ClientHttp2HeaderValidator::transformRequestHeaders/ResponseHeaders()`
